title: PRIVACY READINESS
description: S1 CONTROLLED PUBLIC BETA PRIVACY SUMMARY

introduction.title: 01. S1 PRIVACY SUMMARY
introduction.content: This page summarizes the S1 privacy posture and links conceptually to the full Privacy Policy. It is a controlled-beta candidate, not a final legal approval. Final public seller display, jurisdiction, processor list and retention wording must be approved before go-live.

dataCollection.title: 02. DATA CATEGORIES
dataCollection.content: CyberVPN processes account, authentication, Telegram/OAuth linking, payment, subscription, wallet, provisioning, support, security, audit, notification, webhook and observability data needed to operate the beta. The exact public list must match the deployed backend, Remnawave, provider and support evidence.

noLogs.title: 03. NO-LOGS LIMITS
noLogs.content: CyberVPN does not intend to store browsing content or raw VPN traffic content in app/backend systems. Do not treat this as a final audited no-logs claim: IP address, user-agent, timestamps, provider IDs, support records and provisioning metadata may exist in security, payment, support, node or observability systems until validated.

encryption.title: 04. SECURITY CONTROLS
encryption.content: S1 security relies on hashed passwords, hashed refresh tokens, encrypted selected secrets/tokens where implemented, production admin 2FA, RBAC, audit logs, private PostgreSQL, private Valkey/Redis, private Remnawave API and HTTPS-only public ingress. Stronger crypto or no-logs claims require evidence.

thirdParties.title: 05. PROVIDER BOUNDARIES
thirdParties.content: S1 may use payment providers, Telegram, Google/GitHub OAuth, Remnawave, VPN node hosts, email/support tooling and observability providers. Each enabled provider needs privacy/refund wording, credentials, webhook/status evidence and PII controls before public launch.
